title: Pydantic data models for core domain
description:
Create src/horse_fish/models.py with Pydantic v2 models:

1. AgentSlot — id: str, name: str, runtime: str (claude|copilot|pi|opencode), model: str, capability: str (builder|scout|reviewer|lead), state: AgentState (idle|busy|dead), pid: int | None, tmux_session: str | None, worktree_path: str | None, branch: str | None, task_id: str | None, started_at: datetime | None, idle_since: datetime | None

2. AgentState — str enum: idle, busy, dead

3. Subtask — id: str, description: str, agent: str | None, deps: list[str], files_hint: list[str], state: SubtaskState (pending|running|done|failed|escalated), result: SubtaskResult | None

4. SubtaskState — str enum: pending, running, done, failed, escalated

5. SubtaskResult — subtask_id: str, success: bool, output: str, diff: str, duration_seconds: float

6. Run — id: str, task: str, state: RunState (planning|executing|reviewing|merging|completed|failed), subtasks: list[Subtask], created_at: datetime, completed_at: datetime | None

7. RunState — str enum: planning, executing, reviewing, merging, completed, failed

Use Pydantic BaseModel with sensible defaults. Add factory methods Run.create(task) and Subtask.create(description).

Also create tests/test_models.py with tests for model creation, serialization, state transitions.
closeReason: Created src/horse_fish/models.py with all 7 Pydantic v2 models (AgentSlot, AgentState, Subtask, SubtaskState, SubtaskResult, Run, RunState) and factory methods. 20 tests in tests/test_models.py, all passing, ruff clean.

title: SQLite store foundation
description:
Create src/horse_fish/store/db.py with a thin SQLite wrapper:

1. class Store:
   - __init__(self, db_path: str | Path) — opens/creates SQLite DB with WAL mode
   - execute(sql, params) — execute a query
   - fetchone(sql, params) — return one row as dict
   - fetchall(sql, params) — return all rows as list[dict]
   - close() — close connection
   - __enter__/__exit__ — context manager
   - migrate() — run all migrations in order (check schema_version table)

2. Migrations system:
   - Schema version tracking via a schema_version table
   - migrations list in db.py with (version: int, sql: str) tuples
   - Initial migration (v1): create tables for agents, runs, subtasks, mail

3. Tables (v1 migration):
   - agents: id TEXT PK, name TEXT, runtime TEXT, model TEXT, capability TEXT, state TEXT, pid INTEGER, tmux_session TEXT, worktree_path TEXT, branch TEXT, task_id TEXT, started_at TEXT, idle_since TEXT
   - runs: id TEXT PK, task TEXT, state TEXT, created_at TEXT, completed_at TEXT
   - subtasks: id TEXT PK, run_id TEXT FK, description TEXT, agent TEXT, deps TEXT (JSON array), files_hint TEXT (JSON array), state TEXT, result_output TEXT, result_diff TEXT, result_duration REAL, result_success INTEGER
   - mail: id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, from_agent TEXT, to_agent TEXT, msg_type TEXT, subject TEXT, body TEXT, created_at TEXT, read INTEGER DEFAULT 0

Also create tests/test_store.py with tests using tmp_path fixture for in-memory or temp DB.
closeReason: Implemented Store class with WAL SQLite, execute/fetchone/fetchall, context manager, migrate() with schema_version tracking. v1 migration creates agents, runs, subtasks, mail tables. 10 passing tests.

title: Tmux agent manager
description:
Create src/horse_fish/agents/tmux.py — manages agent processes in tmux panes:

1. class TmuxManager:
   - async spawn(name: str, command: str, cwd: str, env: dict[str, str] | None = None) -> int
     Build inline 'export K=V && command' string, run 'tmux new-session -d -s {name} -c {cwd} {wrapped_cmd}'
     Return PID from 'tmux list-panes -t {name} -F #{pane_pid}'
   
   - async send_keys(session_name: str, text: str) -> None
     Run 'tmux send-keys -t {session_name} {escaped_text} Enter'
   
   - async capture_pane(session_name: str) -> str | None
     Run 'tmux capture-pane -t {session_name} -p', return content or None if session dead
   
   - async kill_session(session_name: str) -> None
     Run 'tmux kill-session -t {session_name}', ignore errors if already dead
   
   - async list_sessions() -> list[str]
     Run 'tmux list-sessions -F #{session_name}', return session names
   
   - async is_alive(session_name: str) -> bool
     Check if session exists in list_sessions

All methods use asyncio.create_subprocess_exec. Handle edge cases: tmux server not running, session already exists, session died.

Also create src/horse_fish/agents/runtime.py with:

2. class RuntimeAdapter (Protocol):
   - runtime_id: str
   - def build_spawn_command(model: str) -> str
   - def build_env() -> dict[str, str]

3. class ClaudeRuntime(RuntimeAdapter) — 'claude --print -p' or interactive 'claude'
4. class CopilotRuntime(RuntimeAdapter) — 'copilot --model {model} --allow-all-tools'
5. class PiRuntime(RuntimeAdapter) — 'pi --model {model}'
6. class OpenCodeRuntime(RuntimeAdapter) — 'opencode -m {model}'

7. RUNTIME_REGISTRY: dict[str, RuntimeAdapter] mapping 'claude', 'copilot', 'pi', 'opencode' to instances

Create tests/test_tmux.py with tests (mock subprocess calls, don't require actual tmux).
closeReason: Implemented tmux manager, runtime adapters, and mocked tests with validation passing.

title: Git worktree manager
description:
Create src/horse_fish/agents/worktree.py — manages git worktrees for agent isolation:

1. class WorktreeManager:
   - __init__(self, repo_root: str, base_dir: str = '.horse-fish/worktrees')
   
   - async create(name: str, base_branch: str = 'main') -> WorktreeInfo
     Create branch 'horse-fish/{name}' and worktree at '{base_dir}/{name}'
     Run: git worktree add {path} -b {branch} {base_branch}
     Return WorktreeInfo(path, branch, name)
   
   - async merge(name: str, auto_commit: bool = True) -> bool
     If auto_commit: stage and commit any uncommitted changes in worktree (with --no-verify)
     Run: git checkout main && git merge --no-ff {branch}
     Return True on success, False on conflict
   
   - async remove(name: str) -> None
     Run: git worktree remove {path} --force
     Run: git branch -D {branch}
   
   - async list_worktrees() -> list[WorktreeInfo]
     Run: git worktree list --porcelain, parse output
   
   - async cleanup(max_age_hours: int = 24) -> int
     Remove worktrees older than max_age_hours
     Run: git worktree prune
     Return count of removed worktrees
   
   - async get_diff(name: str) -> str
     Run: git -C {worktree_path} diff HEAD
     Return diff string

2. WorktreeInfo — Pydantic model: path: str, branch: str, name: str, created_at: datetime

All git operations via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec. Handle: branch already exists (delete and recreate), worktree path exists (remove first), merge conflicts.

Create tests/test_worktree.py with tests using tmp_path and git init for isolated test repos.
closeReason: Implemented WorktreeManager with async git operations (create, merge, remove, list, cleanup, diff). 15 tests passing, lint clean.

title: Build Agent Pool
description:
Create src/horse_fish/agents/pool.py — AgentPool class that wires together TmuxManager, WorktreeManager, RuntimeAdapter (RUNTIME_REGISTRY), and Store to manage agent lifecycle.

API methods:
- spawn(name, runtime, model, capability) -> AgentSlot: lookup runtime in RUNTIME_REGISTRY, create worktree, spawn tmux session with runtime command, persist AgentSlot to store, return slot
- send_task(agent_id, prompt) -> None: send_keys to agent's tmux session
- check_status(agent_id) -> AgentState: is_alive check, update state if dead, return state
- collect_result(agent_id) -> SubtaskResult: capture_pane + get_diff from worktree, build SubtaskResult
- release(agent_id) -> None: kill tmux session, remove worktree, update store
- list_agents() -> list[AgentSlot]: fetch from store
- cleanup() -> int: release dead/idle agents, cleanup old worktrees

Tests in tests/test_pool.py — mock tmux/worktree/store, test lifecycle transitions, error handling.

Imports: Store from horse_fish.store.db, TmuxManager from horse_fish.agents.tmux, WorktreeManager from horse_fish.agents.worktree, RUNTIME_REGISTRY from horse_fish.agents.runtime, AgentSlot/AgentState/SubtaskResult from horse_fish.models.

Conventions: async, Pydantic models, ruff py312 line-length 120, pytest-asyncio.
closeReason: Implemented AgentPool in src/horse_fish/agents/pool.py with all 7 API methods (spawn, send_task, check_status, collect_result, release, list_agents, cleanup). 12 pool tests + 65 total tests pass. Lint clean.

title: Build Validation Gates
description:
Create src/horse_fish/validation/gates.py and src/horse_fish/validation/__init__.py — ValidationGates class that runs quality checks against a worktree path before merge.

Data model (use dataclass, not Pydantic — keep it lightweight):
@dataclass
class GateResult:
    gate: str           # 'ruff-check' | 'ruff-format' | 'pytest' | 'compile'
    passed: bool
    output: str
    duration_seconds: float

API methods:
- __init__(gates: list[str] | None = None): default gates = ['compile', 'ruff-check', 'pytest']
- run_all(worktree_path: str | Path) -> list[GateResult]: run all gates sequentially, return all results (no short-circuit)
- run_gate(gate: str, worktree_path: str | Path) -> GateResult: run single gate
- all_passed(results: list[GateResult]) -> bool

Gate implementations (each runs subprocess in worktree dir):
- compile: python -m py_compile on all .py files (glob src/**/*.py tests/**/*.py)
- ruff-check: ruff check src/ tests/
- pytest: pytest tests/

Tests in tests/test_validation.py — use real tmp_path with sample Python files, test pass/fail for each gate, run_all aggregation, all_passed helper.

Conventions: async, ruff py312 line-length 120, pytest-asyncio.
closeReason: Implemented ValidationGates with compile, ruff-check, pytest gates. GateResult dataclass, async subprocess execution, no-short-circuit run_all. 16 new tests, all 69 total passing, lint clean.

title: Build Planner
description:
Create src/horse_fish/planner/decompose.py and src/horse_fish/planner/__init__.py — Planner class that shells out to a runtime CLI to decompose a task into a subtask DAG.

API methods:
- __init__(runtime: str = 'claude', model: str | None = None)
- decompose(task: str, context: str = '') -> list[Subtask]: build prompt, shell out to CLI (e.g. 'claude --print' for claude runtime), parse JSON response, return list of Subtask objects
- _build_prompt(task: str, context: str) -> str: system prompt instructing LLM to return JSON array of {description, deps, files_hint}
- _parse_response(raw: str) -> list[Subtask]: extract JSON from response (handle markdown code fences), validate, create Subtask objects via Subtask.create()

CLI command building per runtime:
- claude: claude --print -m <model> '<prompt>'
- copilot: copilot --print --model <model> '<prompt>'
- pi: pi --print --model <model> '<prompt>'
- opencode: opencode --print -m <model> '<prompt>'

Import Subtask from horse_fish.models.

Tests in tests/test_planner.py — mock asyncio.create_subprocess_exec. Test prompt building, JSON parsing (valid response, malformed JSON, empty response, markdown-fenced JSON), Subtask creation.

Conventions: async, ruff py312 line-length 120, pytest-asyncio.
closeReason: Implemented Planner class in src/horse_fish/planner/decompose.py with decompose(), _build_prompt(), _build_command(), _run_cli(), _parse_response(). Tests in tests/test_planner.py (30 passing). Supports claude/copilot/pi/opencode runtimes, JSON parsing with markdown fence handling.

title: ID-based deps for blocker-aware dispatch
description:
Switch _deps_met() in orchestrator/engine.py from description-based matching to ID-based matching. Also add _resolve_deps() to convert planner output.

WHAT TO DO:
1. In src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py, change _deps_met (line ~336) to match by subtask ID instead of description:
   - Old: done_descriptions = {s.description for s in run.subtasks if s.state == SubtaskState.done}; return all(dep in done_descriptions for dep in subtask.deps)
   - New: done_ids = {s.id for s in run.subtasks if s.state == SubtaskState.done}; return all(dep in done_ids for dep in subtask.deps)

2. Add a new static method _resolve_deps(subtasks) to Orchestrator class that converts description-based deps to ID-based deps:
   - Build a dict desc_to_id mapping description->id for all subtasks
   - For each subtask, replace each dep with desc_to_id[dep] if found, else keep as-is
   
3. In _plan() method (line ~157), call self._resolve_deps(subtasks) BEFORE setting run.subtasks = subtasks

4. Update tests in tests/test_orchestrator.py:
   - Change sample_subtasks fixture: deps=['subtask-1'] instead of deps=['Implement user model']
   - Change test_execute_respects_dag_deps: deps=['subtask-1'] instead of deps=['Implement base']
   - Change test_deps_met_all_done: deps=['s1'] instead of deps=['Task 1']
   - Change test_deps_met_not_done: deps=['s1'] instead of deps=['Task 1']
   - Change test_deps_met_partial: deps=['s1', 's2'] instead of deps=['Task 1', 'Task 2']
   - Add test_deps_met_by_id: subtasks with ID-based deps, verify True when done
   - Add test_resolve_deps_converts_descriptions_to_ids: verify conversion works
   - Add test_resolve_deps_keeps_unknown_deps: verify unknown deps kept as-is

Run pytest tests/test_orchestrator.py -v to verify all tests pass.
closeReason: Implemented ID-based dependency matching in Orchestrator. Changed _deps_met() to match by subtask ID, added _resolve_deps() to convert planner output from description-based to ID-based deps. Updated tests and added 3 new tests. All 39 tests pass.

title: CLI hf logs command
description:
Add a new 'logs' command to the horse-fish CLI that shows agent tmux output.

WHAT TO DO:
1. In src/horse_fish/cli.py, add a new command after the merge command:

@main.command()
@click.option('--agent', default=None, help='Show logs for a specific agent')
@click.option('--lines', default=20, type=int, help='Number of lines to show per agent')
def logs(agent, lines):
    """View agent tmux output."""
    tmux = TmuxManager()
    if agent:
        output = asyncio.run(tmux.capture_pane(agent))
        if output is None:
            click.echo(f"Agent '{agent}' not found or no output available.")
            return
        tail = '\n'.join(output.splitlines()[-lines:]) if output.strip() else '(empty)'
        click.echo(f'--- {agent} ---')
        click.echo(tail)
    else:
        sessions = asyncio.run(tmux.list_sessions())
        hf_sessions = [s for s in sessions if s.startswith('hf-')]
        if not hf_sessions:
            click.echo('No active horse-fish agents.')
            return
        for session in hf_sessions:
            output = asyncio.run(tmux.capture_pane(session))
            if output is None:
                continue
            tail = '\n'.join(output.splitlines()[-lines:]) if output.strip() else '(empty)'
            click.echo(f'--- {session} ---')
            click.echo(tail)
            click.echo()

2. Add 4 tests to tests/test_cli.py:
   - test_logs_lists_all_sessions: mock TmuxManager, list_sessions returns ['hf-agent-1','hf-agent-2','unrelated'], verify only hf- sessions shown
   - test_logs_single_agent: mock capture_pane, invoke with --agent hf-agent-1, verify output shown
   - test_logs_agent_not_found: mock capture_pane returns None, verify 'not found' or 'no output' message
   - test_logs_no_sessions: mock list_sessions returns ['unrelated'], verify 'No active' message

Use from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch and @patch('horse_fish.cli.TmuxManager') pattern.

Run pytest tests/test_cli.py -v to verify all tests pass.
closeReason: Added 'hf logs' CLI command with --agent and --lines options to view agent tmux output. Added 4 tests. All quality gates passed.

title: End-to-end integration test with real tmux
description:
Create tests/test_e2e.py with real subprocess tests proving tmux + worktree + agent flow works.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Create tests/test_e2e.py with these contents:

- Import: asyncio, os, subprocess, tempfile, Path, pytest, AsyncMock
- Import: AgentPool, TmuxManager, WorktreeManager, Subtask, SubtaskState, Store
- Add _tmux_available() helper that checks subprocess.run(['tmux', '-V'])
- Add pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(not _tmux_available(), reason='tmux not available')

- Fixture tmp_repo(tmp_path): create temp git repo with git init, config user, initial commit of README.md
- Fixture store(tmp_path): Store(tmp_path/'test.db'), call migrate()
- Fixture tmux(): return TmuxManager()
- Fixture worktrees(tmp_repo): return WorktreeManager(str(tmp_repo))
- Fixture pool(store, tmux, worktrees): return AgentPool(store, tmux, worktrees)

Test 1 - test_e2e_single_subtask_creates_file:
  - Mark with @pytest.mark.asyncio and @pytest.mark.timeout(30)
  - Spawn agent via pool.spawn(name='hf-e2e-test', runtime='claude', model='test', capability='builder')
  - Send shell command via pool._tmux.send_keys that creates a file, git adds, and commits in the worktree
  - Poll pool.collect_result() in a loop (up to 10 iterations with asyncio.sleep(1))
  - Assert result.diff is truthy and result.success is True
  - Cleanup with pool.release(slot.id)

Test 2 - test_e2e_worktree_isolation:
  - Mark with @pytest.mark.asyncio and @pytest.mark.timeout(30)
  - Spawn agent via pool.spawn
  - Assert slot.worktree_path is not None and differs from tmp_repo
  - Assert Path(slot.worktree_path).exists()
  - Assert main repo still has README.md
  - Cleanup with pool.release(slot.id)

Run pytest tests/test_e2e.py -v --timeout=30 to verify tests pass (or skip if no tmux).
closeReason: Created tests/test_e2e.py with e2e integration tests for tmux+worktree+agent flow. 2 tests passing.

title: Ready detection for agent runtimes
description:
Add ready detection so AgentPool waits for the runtime to show its prompt before sending tasks.

WHAT TO DO:

1. Modify src/horse_fish/agents/runtime.py:
   - Remove frozen=True from all runtime dataclasses (change to just @dataclass(slots=True))
   - Add ready_pattern and ready_timeout_seconds fields to each runtime:
     ClaudeRuntime: ready_pattern=r'[❯>]\s*$', ready_timeout_seconds=30
     CopilotRuntime: ready_pattern=r'[>]\s*$', ready_timeout_seconds=60
     PiRuntime: ready_pattern=r'[>›]\s*$', ready_timeout_seconds=30
     OpenCodeRuntime: ready_pattern=r'[>]\s*$', ready_timeout_seconds=30
   - Add to RuntimeAdapter protocol: ready_pattern: str, ready_timeout_seconds: int
   - Update PiRuntime.build_env() to return {'DASHSCOPE_API_KEY': os.environ.get('DASHSCOPE_API_KEY', '')} if key exists, else empty dict. Import os at top.

2. Modify src/horse_fish/agents/pool.py:
   - Add imports: import asyncio, import re
   - Add method _wait_for_ready(self, slot: AgentSlot) -> None to AgentPool:
     - Gets adapter from RUNTIME_REGISTRY[slot.runtime]
     - Compiles adapter.ready_pattern with re.MULTILINE
     - Polls self._tmux.capture_pane(slot.tmux_session) every 1s
     - Returns when pattern matches
     - If elapsed >= timeout: kill session, remove worktree, raise RuntimeError('Agent ... did not become ready within Ns')
   - In spawn() method, call await self._wait_for_ready(slot) AFTER creating the AgentSlot but BEFORE the INSERT INTO agents SQL

3. Update tests/test_pool.py:
   - IMPORTANT: Every existing test that calls pool.spawn() needs tmux.capture_pane mocked to return a string matching the ready pattern. Add tmux.capture_pane = AsyncMock(return_value='Ready\n❯ \n') for claude tests, or 'Ready\n> \n' for copilot/pi/opencode tests.
   - For collect_result tests that already mock capture_pane, use side_effect list: first value for ready detection, second for the actual test.
   - Add 3 new tests:
     a) test_spawn_waits_for_ready_pattern: mock capture_pane side_effect=['Loading...\n', 'Loading...\n❯ \n'], verify capture_pane called >= 2 times
     b) test_spawn_raises_on_ready_timeout: mock capture_pane always returns 'Loading...\n', temporarily set runtime ready_timeout_seconds=2, verify raises RuntimeError matching 'ready'
     c) test_spawn_works_with_pi_ready_pattern: mock capture_pane returns 'Welcome to Pi\n> \n', verify slot created ok

Run pytest tests/test_pool.py -v to verify all tests pass.
closeReason: Added ready detection for agent runtimes: runtime dataclasses now have ready_pattern and ready_timeout_seconds ClassVars, AgentPool._wait_for_ready() polls tmux output before persisting slot, 15 tests pass

title: Agent prompt template module

WHAT TO DO:

def build_prompt(task: str, worktree_path: str, branch: str, project_context: str | None = None) -> str:

    - Intro: 'You are an agent in the horse-fish swarm working in an isolated git worktree.'

   - In send_task(), wrap the prompt: full_prompt = build_prompt(task=prompt, worktree_path=slot.worktree_path or '', branch=slot.branch or '', project_context=self._project_context)

   - Pass to AgentPool: pool = AgentPool(store, tmux, worktrees, project_context=project_context)

5. Update tests/test_pool.py:

title: Create SmartPlanner with complexity classification

Create src/horse_fish/planner/smart.py with SmartPlanner class:
- Wraps existing Planner from horse_fish.planner.decompose
- decompose(task, context) -> tuple[list[Subtask], TaskComplexity]
- _classify(task, context, lessons) -> TaskComplexity: sends classification prompt to LLM via planner._build_command + planner._run_cli. Prompt asks LLM to reply SOLO/TRIO/SQUAD.
- _parse_complexity(raw) -> TaskComplexity: parse LLM response, default SOLO if unparseable
- _strip_ceremony(subtasks) -> list[Subtask]: remove subtasks matching ceremony patterns (commit, test, review, format, lint, verify, etc.)
- _get_lessons(task) -> str: query LessonStore if available
- Caps: SOLO=1, TRIO=3, SQUAD=8 max subtasks
- Fallback: if decompose fails or returns empty or all ceremony -> single subtask wrapping whole task

Depends on: horse_fish.planner.decompose (Planner), horse_fish.memory.lessons (LessonStore), horse_fish.models (Subtask, TaskComplexity)

title: Fix retry deadlock in engine.py
description:
## Bug

In `src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py`, the `_execute()` method has a retry deadlock.

`_check_stalls()` (line 157) can move a running subtask back to `pending` state and release the agent, but `_execute()` never decrements `active_count` when this happens. At the concurrency limit, a retried task becomes permanently undispatchable because `active_count` still counts the stalled agent.

## Fix Required

In `_execute()` (line 221), after calling `_check_stalls()` on line 307, subtract the returned retry count from `active_count`:

```python
# Line 307 currently:
await self._check_stalls(run, agent_map)

# Should be:
retried = await self._check_stalls(run, agent_map)
active_count -= retried
```

Also: when `_check_stalls` marks a subtask as `failed` (exhausted retries), that also needs to decrement `active_count`. Update `_check_stalls` to track both retried AND failed counts, and return the total count of subtasks that are no longer running.

## Tests

Add tests in `tests/test_engine.py` that verify:
1. When a subtask is retried after stall, `active_count` is correctly decremented so new tasks can be dispatched
2. When a subtask fails after exhausting retries, `active_count` is also decremented

Look at existing tests in `tests/test_engine.py` for patterns — they mock the pool, planner, and gates.

## Constraints
- Only modify `src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py` and `tests/test_engine.py`
- Run `pytest tests/test_engine.py -x -q` to verify
- Run `ruff check src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py` to lint
closeReason: Fixed retry deadlock in engine.py: _check_stalls() returns retried+failed count, _execute() decrements active_count. Added 5 tests. All quality gates passed.

title: Fix pool.py task identity and cleanup
description:
## Bug 1: Task identity lost in send_task()

In `src/horse_fish/agents/pool.py`, `send_task()` (line 90) marks the agent as busy but never persists the `task_id` (subtask_id) in the database. The UPDATE on line 100 only sets `state` but not `task_id`.

This means `collect_result()` (line 121) uses `slot.task_id or agent_id` which always falls back to `agent_id` since task_id is never set.

### Fix

Update `send_task()` to accept and persist the subtask_id:

1. Add a `task_id` parameter to `send_task()`: `async def send_task(self, agent_id: str, prompt: str, task_id: str | None = None) -> None:`
2. Update the SQL to also set task_id: `UPDATE agents SET state = ?, task_id = ? WHERE id = ?`

Then in `src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py` line 253, pass the subtask.id:
```python
await self._pool.send_task(slot.id, subtask.description, task_id=subtask.id)
```

**IMPORTANT:** The engine.py change is just adding `task_id=subtask.id` to the existing `send_task` call on line 253. Do NOT modify any other lines in engine.py.

## Bug 2: Incomplete cleanup()

`cleanup()` (line 140) only releases agents in `dead` or `idle` state. Agents that finished work remain `busy` forever, stranding tmux sessions and worktrees. The `hf clean` CLI command promises "Kill all agents" but doesn't deliver.

### Fix

Change line 145 from:
```python
if slot.state in (AgentState.dead, AgentState.idle):
```
to:
```python
if slot.state in (AgentState.dead, AgentState.idle, AgentState.busy):
```

This makes `cleanup()` release ALL agents regardless of state, which is what "kill all agents" should mean.

## Tests

Add/update tests in `tests/test_pool.py`:
1. Test that `send_task` persists the task_id in the database
2. Test that `collect_result` returns the correct subtask_id when task_id is set
3. Test that `cleanup()` also releases busy agents

Look at existing test patterns in `tests/test_pool.py`.

## Constraints
- Primary changes in `src/horse_fish/agents/pool.py`
- Minimal change in `src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py` (only add task_id param to send_task call)
- Run `pytest tests/test_pool.py -x -q` to verify
- Run `ruff check src/horse_fish/agents/pool.py` to lint
closeReason: Fixed pool.py task identity and cleanup bugs: send_task() now persists task_id, cleanup() releases busy agents. Added 4 tests. All quality gates pass.

title: Add BashRuntime and fix e2e tests
description:
## Bug

In `tests/test_e2e.py`, the e2e tests spawn `runtime="claude"` but the docstring says they use "a bash shell instead of a real agent runtime". The tests patch `_wait_for_ready` so they don't need claude to actually be ready, but the spawn builds a claude command via the runtime registry. If claude isn't installed, the tmux pane has a dead/error shell.

The right fix: add a proper BashRuntime adapter so tests can spawn `runtime="bash"`.

## Step 1: Add BashRuntime to runtime.py

In `src/horse_fish/agents/runtime.py`, add a BashRuntime adapter:

```python
@dataclass(slots=True)
class BashRuntime:
    """Adapter for plain bash shell — used in testing."""

    runtime_id: ClassVar[str] = "bash"
    ready_pattern: ClassVar[str] = r"\$\s*$"
    ready_timeout_seconds: ClassVar[int] = 5

    def build_spawn_command(self, model: str) -> str:
        return "bash"

    def build_env(self) -> dict[str, str]:
        return {}
```

Add it to RUNTIME_REGISTRY:
```python
RUNTIME_REGISTRY: dict[str, RuntimeAdapter] = {
    ClaudeRuntime.runtime_id: ClaudeRuntime(),
    CopilotRuntime.runtime_id: CopilotRuntime(),
    PiRuntime.runtime_id: PiRuntime(),
    OpenCodeRuntime.runtime_id: OpenCodeRuntime(),
    BashRuntime.runtime_id: BashRuntime(),
}
```

## Step 2: Fix e2e tests

In `tests/test_e2e.py`:

1. Change both tests from `runtime="claude"` to `runtime="bash"`
2. The tests can still patch `_wait_for_ready` since the bash prompt pattern might not match in all terminal configs
3. Update the docstring if needed to match what the test actually does

Specifically:
- Line 91: change `runtime="claude"` to `runtime="bash"`
- Line 129: change `runtime="claude"` to `runtime="bash"`

## Step 3: Add BashRuntime test

In `tests/test_runtime.py`, add a test for BashRuntime:
- Test that `build_spawn_command` returns "bash"
- Test that `build_env` returns empty dict
- Test that it's in RUNTIME_REGISTRY

Look at existing runtime tests for the pattern.

## Constraints
- Modify `src/horse_fish/agents/runtime.py`, `tests/test_e2e.py`, `tests/test_runtime.py`
- Do NOT modify pool.py or engine.py
- Run `pytest tests/test_runtime.py tests/test_e2e.py -x -q` to verify
- Run `ruff check src/horse_fish/agents/runtime.py` to lint
closeReason: Added BashRuntime adapter to runtime.py with runtime_id='bash', ready_pattern for bash prompt, build_spawn_command returning 'bash'. Updated e2e tests to use runtime='bash' instead of 'claude'. Added comprehensive BashRuntime tests. All 16 tests pass, ruff check and format pass.

title: SQLite persistence for runs and subtasks
description:
## Goal

Add SQLite persistence for Run and Subtask state so the TUI dashboard can observe orchestrator progress. Currently these objects only live in memory.

## Step 1: Add tables to store migration

In `src/horse_fish/store/db.py`, add two new tables in the `migrate()` method:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS runs (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    task TEXT NOT NULL,
    state TEXT NOT NULL,
    complexity TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    completed_at TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS subtasks (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    run_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT NOT NULL,
    state TEXT NOT NULL,
    agent_id TEXT,
    deps TEXT,
    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (run_id) REFERENCES runs(id)
);
```

## Step 2: Add persistence helper methods to Store

Add these methods to the Store class in `src/horse_fish/store/db.py`:

```python
def upsert_run(self, run_id: str, task: str, state: str, complexity: str | None = None,
               created_at: str | None = None, completed_at: str | None = None) -> None:
    """Insert or update a run record."""
    self.execute(
        """INSERT INTO runs (id, task, state, complexity, created_at, completed_at)
           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
           ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET state=excluded.state, complexity=excluded.complexity,
           completed_at=excluded.completed_at""",
        (run_id, task, state, complexity, created_at, completed_at),
    )

def upsert_subtask(self, subtask_id: str, run_id: str, description: str, state: str,
                   agent_id: str | None = None, deps: str | None = None,
                   retry_count: int = 0, created_at: str | None = None) -> None:
    """Insert or update a subtask record."""
    self.execute(
        """INSERT INTO subtasks (id, run_id, description, state, agent_id, deps, retry_count, created_at)
           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
           ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET state=excluded.state, agent_id=excluded.agent_id,
           deps=excluded.deps, retry_count=excluded.retry_count""",
        (subtask_id, run_id, description, state, agent_id, deps, retry_count, created_at),
    )
```

## Step 3: Wire orchestrator to persist state

In `src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py`, add a `_store` parameter to `__init__` (optional, `Store | None = None`).

Add a `_persist_run` and `_persist_subtask` helper:

```python
def _persist_run(self, run: Run) -> None:
    if not self._store:
        return
    self._store.upsert_run(
        run_id=run.id, task=run.task, state=run.state.value,
        complexity=run.complexity,
        created_at=run.created_at.isoformat() if run.created_at else datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
        completed_at=run.completed_at.isoformat() if run.completed_at else None,
    )

def _persist_subtask(self, subtask: Subtask, run_id: str) -> None:
    if not self._store:
        return
    import json
    self._store.upsert_subtask(
        subtask_id=subtask.id, run_id=run_id, description=subtask.description,
        state=subtask.state.value, agent_id=subtask.agent,
        deps=json.dumps(subtask.deps) if subtask.deps else None,
        retry_count=subtask.retry_count,
        created_at=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
    )
```

Call `_persist_run` at these points:
- In `run()`: after `Run.create()` (initial insert)
- In `run()`: after each `handler(run)` call (state transition update)
- In `run()`: after setting `completed_at` (terminal state)

Call `_persist_subtask` at these points:
- In `_plan()`: after setting `run.subtasks` (initial insert for each subtask)
- In `_execute()`: after changing any subtask state (dispatch, completion, failure)
- In `_check_stalls()`: after retry or failure state change
- In `_review()`: after gate pass/fail

Then in `src/horse_fish/cli.py`, pass the store to the Orchestrator constructor:

```python
orchestrator = Orchestrator(
    ...
    store=store,  # Add this
)
```

Check the existing Orchestrator.__init__ signature and add `store: Store | None = None` as a parameter.

## Step 4: Check Run model has created_at

Check `src/horse_fish/models.py` — the Run model needs a `created_at` field. If it doesn't have one, add it:
```python
created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(UTC))
```

## Tests

Add tests in `tests/test_store.py`:
1. Test that `migrate()` creates runs and subtasks tables
2. Test `upsert_run` insert and update
3. Test `upsert_subtask` insert and update

Add tests in `tests/test_engine.py`:
4. Test that orchestrator persists run state to SQLite when store is provided
5. Test that orchestrator persists subtask state changes to SQLite

## Constraints
- Modify: `src/horse_fish/store/db.py`, `src/horse_fish/orchestrator/engine.py`, `src/horse_fish/cli.py`, `src/horse_fish/models.py` (if needed)
- Tests in: `tests/test_store.py`, `tests/test_engine.py`
- Run `pytest tests/test_store.py tests/test_engine.py -x -q` to verify
- Run `ruff check src/ --fix` to lint
- Use `pytest` not `bun test` for testing
closeReason: Added SQLite persistence for Run and Subtask state. Updated db.py schema with NOT NULL constraints and new columns (complexity, agent_id, retry_count, created_at). Added upsert_run/upsert_subtask methods to Store. Wired orchestrator to persist state at all lifecycle points. Added 11 tests. All quality gates passed.

title: TUI dashboard widgets
description:
## Goal

Build Textual TUI widgets for the horse-fish dashboard. These are read-only display components that render data from SQLite.

## Prerequisites

The `textual` package needs to be available. Install it first:
```bash
pip install "textual>=1.0"
```

## Step 1: Create widget file

Create `src/horse_fish/dashboard/__init__.py` (empty file).
Create `src/horse_fish/dashboard/widgets.py` with these widgets:

### PipelineBar (Static widget)

Shows the orchestrator pipeline phase as a horizontal indicator:

```
plan → [EXECUTING] → review → merge → learn     Run: abc-123
```

- Takes `run_state: str` and `run_id: str` as reactive properties
- Highlights the current phase (bold/colored)
- Uses Textual's `Static` or `Label` as base class
- Phases: planning, executing, reviewing, merging, completed, failed

```python
from textual.widgets import Static
from textual.reactive import reactive

class PipelineBar(Static):
    run_state: reactive[str] = reactive("idle")
    run_id: reactive[str] = reactive("")

    def render(self) -> str:
        phases = ["planning", "executing", "reviewing", "merging"]
        parts = []
        for phase in phases:
            if phase == self.run_state:
                parts.append(f"[bold green][{phase.upper()}][/]")
            else:
                parts.append(phase)
        pipeline = " → ".join(parts)
        if self.run_state == "completed":
            pipeline += " → [bold green]COMPLETED[/]"
        elif self.run_state == "failed":
            pipeline += " → [bold red]FAILED[/]"
        run_info = f"  Run: {self.run_id[:12]}" if self.run_id else ""
        return pipeline + run_info
```

### AgentTable (DataTable)

Shows all agent slots from SQLite:

| NAME | RUNTIME | STATE | TASK |
|------|---------|-------|------|

- Wraps Textual `DataTable`
- Columns: name, runtime, state, task_id
- Highlight row = selected agent (drives log pane)
- Emits a custom message `AgentSelected(agent_tmux_session: str)` on cursor change

```python
from textual.widgets import DataTable
from textual.message import Message

class AgentTable(DataTable):
    class AgentSelected(Message):
        def __init__(self, tmux_session: str) -> None:
            self.tmux_session = tmux_session
            super().__init__()

    def on_data_table_cursor_changed(self, event) -> None:
        # Get the tmux_session from the selected row and post AgentSelected
        ...
```

### SubtaskTable (DataTable)

Shows all subtasks from SQLite:

| DESCRIPTION | STATE | AGENT |
|------------|-------|-------|

- Wraps Textual `DataTable`
- Columns: description (truncated to 40 chars), state, agent_id
- Color-code states: done=green, running=yellow, pending=dim, failed=red

### AgentLog (RichLog)

Shows live tmux capture output for the selected agent:

```python
from textual.widgets import RichLog

class AgentLog(RichLog):
    def update_log(self, output: str) -> None:
        self.clear()
        self.write(output)
```

## Step 2: Tests

Create `tests/test_dashboard_widgets.py`:

1. Test PipelineBar renders correctly for each state
2. Test PipelineBar shows run_id
3. Test AgentTable can be populated with agent data
4. Test SubtaskTable can be populated with subtask data

Use Textual's async test helpers:
```python
from textual.app import App, ComposeResult

class WidgetTestApp(App):
    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        yield PipelineBar()

async def test_pipeline_bar_renders():
    async with WidgetTestApp().run_test() as pilot:
        bar = pilot.app.query_one(PipelineBar)
        bar.run_state = "executing"
        # verify render contains EXECUTING
```

## Constraints
- Create: `src/horse_fish/dashboard/__init__.py`, `src/horse_fish/dashboard/widgets.py`, `tests/test_dashboard_widgets.py`
- Do NOT create `app.py` — that's a separate task
- Do NOT modify `cli.py`, `engine.py`, or `store/db.py`
- Run `pytest tests/test_dashboard_widgets.py -x -q` to verify
- Run `ruff check src/horse_fish/dashboard/ --fix` to lint
- Use `pytest` not `bun test`
closeReason: Implemented TUI dashboard widgets: PipelineBar (pipeline phase indicator with reactive state), AgentTable (DataTable with AgentSelected message), SubtaskTable (DataTable with state styling), AgentLog (RichLog for tmux output). 16 tests pass, quality gates passed.
